name: PennyMac Financial Services, Inc.
business: PennyMac Financial Services, Inc. is a specialty financial services firm with a comprehensive mortgage platform focused on the production and servicing of U.S. residential mortgage loans. It operates through two segments: production (loan origination, acquisition, and sale) and servicing (loan servicing and subservicing). It sells loans to government-sponsored entities and government agencies and provides subservicing for PennyMac Mortgage Investment Trust and non-affiliates.
sellsTo: government agencies (Ginnie Mae, FHA, VA, USDA)

exposures.sentence: providing products and services that leverage innovative technologies to effectively and efficiently support our customers.

Cybersecurity risks, cyber incidents and technology failures may adversely affect our business by causing a disruption to our operations, a compromise or corruption of our confidential information or the personal information of our customers, and/or damage to our business relationships, all of which could negatively impact our financial results.
The development, proliferation and use of artificial intelligence could give rise to legal and/or regulatory action, damage our reputation or otherwise materially harm our business.

catalysts.event: Became initial purchaser of all loans from correspondent sellers and began transferring agreed upon volumes of conventional loans to PMT.
catalysts.sentence: Beginning July 1, 2025, we became the initial purchaser of all loans from correspondent sellers and now transfer agreed upon volumes of conventional loans to PMT.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. residential mortgage market debt outstanding was approximately $14.7 trillion as of December 31, 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: The U.S. residential mortgage market is one of the largest financial markets in the world, with approximately $14.7 trillion of debt outstanding as of December 31, 2025.

tamClaims.claim: First lien mortgage loan origination volume was approximately $1.9 trillion in 2025 and expected to increase to $2.3 trillion in 2026.
tamClaims.sentence: According to mortgage industry economists, first lien mortgage loan origination volume was approximately $1.9 trillion in 2025 and is expected to increase to $2.3 trillion in 2026.
